steps.title: Locate the marked angle
steps.content: The figure marks a $135^\circ$ angle where line $k$ intersects line $n$. The angle labeled $s$ is at that same intersection, directly opposite the marked angle.

steps.title: Use vertical angles
steps.content: When two lines intersect, opposite angles are vertical angles, and vertical angles have equal measures. Since $s$ is opposite the marked $135^\circ$ angle, the value of $s$ is also $135^\circ$.

steps.title: Check the other labels
steps.content: The angles adjacent to the marked $135^\circ$ angle are supplementary to it, so each adjacent angle measures $45^\circ$. The labels $q$, $r$, and $t$ are not the angle that is vertical to the marked $135^\circ$ angle.

steps.title: Choose the true statement
steps.content: The true statement is that $s=135$. The answer is C.
